nodes.label: Two-Brain SCFF + Namer Hybrid (draft 6.0)
nodes.rationale: The rebuilt architecture that replaced attribution-based hierarchical diffusion: ~80% a cheap unsupervised forward-only SCFF bulk that organizes structure label-free, ~20% a precise namer that maps that structure to real labels - the namer turned out closed-form (SLDA/RanPAC), needing no gradient descent at all, so the committed chip contains no global backward pass.

nodes.label: Direction Is the One Expensive Thing (the bet)
nodes.rationale: Working out how much a weight matters (magnitude) is cheap - the substrate measures it as physical current for free. Working out which way it should move (direction/sign) is what costs a backward pass, a transpose, a chain of dependencies. Draft 6.0 pays for direction once (the namer) and gets everything else cheaply (the SCFF bulk).
